pattern: For each element treated as the minimum of a window, the widest stretch over which it stays the minimum is bounded by the nearest smaller elements on each side, and that maximal width is the strongest test of the per-length threshold.
hints: Rewrite the condition: a window of length k is valid when its minimum times k exceeds the threshold, so fix the minimum and you want the longest window it governs.
The longest window in which a given element is the minimum runs between the previous strictly-smaller and next strictly-smaller elements.
A monotonic stack yields those boundaries in linear time; for each element check whether its value times its span beats the threshold.

edge_inputs.description: unique valid subarray in the middle
many valid sizes including a single strong element
no subarray can meet the threshold
single element that just clears the bar
all equal so any length up to where value*k exceeds threshold works

stress.description: random arrays with a threshold scaled to the values so both feasible and infeasible cases appear
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 120))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 50) for _ in range(n)]
    threshold = rng.randint(1, 60 * n)
    return [nums, threshold]
stress.note: small values keep the O(n^2) oracle fast; threshold range spans easy and impossible
